message.piranport.patchouli_required: Patchouli mod is required to open this book.
piranport.book.name: Piran Port Navigation Manual
piranport.book.landing: A guide to ship rigging, weapons, cooking, aviation, combat buffs, and dungeon sorties. Craft it with a Book and an Aluminum Ingot.
piranport.book.cat.cooking.desc: Crops, processing stations, and naval cuisine.
piranport.book.cat.aviation.desc: Aircraft squadrons, groups, and fire control.
piranport.book.intro.overview.p1: Piran Port is a Warship Girls R themed mod. To begin, mine $(item)Bauxite Ore$() for $(item)Aluminum Ingots$() and collect $(item)Salt$(). Craft a $(item)Ship Core$() — Small, Medium, or Large — and place your weapons inside its GUI.
piranport.book.intro.overview.craft: Three ship core sizes available. Larger cores hold more weapons and have higher load capacity.
piranport.book.intro.transform.p1: $(bold)Shift + Right-click$() a Ship Core to toggle transformation. Your speed decreases based on total equipment weight. Enhancement slots accept $(item)Armor Plates$() for bonus defense and extra weight.
piranport.book.intro.transform.p2: While transformed, $(bold)right-click$() to fire the active weapon. Press $(bold)V$() to cycle between weapon slots. Open the Ship Core GUI (right-click while not transformed) to manage weapons, ammo, and enhancements.
piranport.book.cannons.basics.p1: Craft $(item)Small$(), $(item)Medium$(), or $(item)Large Guns$() and load matching caliber shells. $(item)HE Shells$() cause area explosions. $(item)AP Shells$() deal higher direct damage with no explosion. Place guns in weapon slots and ammo in the ammo bay.
piranport.book.cannons.basics.craft: Small, Medium, and Large guns use different caliber shells. Shells must match the gun.
piranport.book.torpedoes.basics.p1: Torpedo launchers (Twin/Triple/Quad) fire 533mm or 610mm torpedoes in a spread pattern. Torpedoes travel across water surfaces and inflict the $(item)Flooding$() debuff on hit. They self-destruct after 1 minute if they miss.
piranport.book.torpedoes.basics.craft: Twin launcher fires 2 torpedoes in a ±3° spread. Larger launchers spread wider.
piranport.book.cooking.crops.p1: Eight crops can be grown: $(item)Tomato$(), $(item)Soybean$(), $(item)Chili$(), $(item)Onion$(), $(item)Rice$(), $(item)Lettuce$(), $(item)Garlic$(), and $(item)Pineapple$(). Seeds drop from tall grass or wandering traders. Plant on farmland; Rice grows in waterlogged soil.
piranport.book.cooking.stations.p1: $(bold)Stone Mill:$() Right-click to open. Grind crops into flour, powder, and paste. Accepts up to 4 ingredients and produces 1-2 outputs.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Cutting Board:$() Right-click to place an ingredient, then right-click repeatedly to cut. Shift+right-click to retrieve.
piranport.book.cooking.stations.p2: $(bold)Cooking Pot:$() Place over a heat source (fire, campfire, magma, lava). Right-click to open GUI. Add ingredients; the pot cooks automatically when heated. Recipes combine 1-9 inputs into a single food output.
piranport.book.aviation.aircraft.p1: Five aircraft types: $(item)Fighter$() (hovers and fires bullets, 64 rounds), $(item)Dive Bomber$() (dives close and drops a bomb), $(item)Torpedo Bomber$() (fires torpedo at 20-30 blocks range), $(item)Level Bomber$() (drops bombs from 32 blocks above, 8 rounds), and $(item)Recon Squadron$() (see Recon entry).
piranport.book.aviation.aircraft.p2: Press $(bold)[U]$() while transformed to open Groups directly. Assign aircraft slots (left-click), right-click to cycle ammo: $(item)Fighter Ammo$() for fighters, $(item)Aerial Bomb$() for bombers, $(item)Aerial Torpedo$() for torpedo bombers. No ammo assigned = cannot launch.
piranport.book.aviation.fire_control.p1: While transformed: $(bold)[P]$() locks crosshair target (80-block range); $(bold)[O]$() adds target; $(bold)[I]$() cancels all locks. Locked targets shown in top-right HUD. Press $(bold)[Y]$() to toggle battlefield highlight — your aircraft/torpedoes/bombs glow. Only visible to you.
piranport.book.aviation.recon.p1: The $(item)Recon Squadron$() is a non-combat aircraft used for scouting. It carries no weapons or ammo. After launching, press $(bold)[V]$() to enter recon view — your camera follows the aircraft in real time. Press $(bold)[V]$() again to exit. The recon plane force-loads chunks around it so you can see distant terrain. Useful for spotting enemy positions before committing to an attack.
piranport.book.cat.combat.desc: Combat effects, buff foods, and battlefield tactics.
piranport.book.combat.effects.p1: $(bold)Flooding:$() Applied by torpedo hits. Slows and damages the target over time.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Reload Boost:$() Increases ammo resupply speed. Levels I-III available. Obtained from food like $(item)Chicken Tatsuta$() or $(item)Torpedo Juice$().$(br)$(br)$(bold)Evasion:$() Grants a dodge chance (15-35% based on level) while transformed. Obtained from $(item)Tempura$() or $(item)Kvass$().$(br)$(br)$(bold)Flammable:$() Fire hazard debuff when loading aviation fuel (disabled by default in config).
piranport.book.combat.buff_foods.p1: $(item)Chicken Tatsuta$() — $(bold)Reload Boost I$() for 180s.$(br)$(br)$(item)Torpedo Juice$() — $(bold)Hunger II + Reload Boost II + Fire Resistance I$(); returns bottle.$(br)$(br)$(item)Tempura$() — $(bold)Evasion I$() for 180s.$(br)$(br)$(item)Kvass$() — $(bold)Slowness I + Slow Falling I + Evasion II$() for 120s; returns bottle.$(br)$(br)Cook these at the $(item)Cooking Pot$() using standard ingredients.
piranport.book.cooking.salt_rice.p1: $(bold)Salt Evaporation:$() Place a $(item)Water Source$() block directly above a lit $(item)Furnace$(). After 10 seconds, the water converts to a $(item)Salt Block$(). Flowing water above a furnace produces $(item)Salt Chips$() instead.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Rice Paddies:$() $(item)Rice$() must be planted on waterlogged farmland — place water and plant rice seeds on the flooded block. If the water is removed, the crop breaks.$(br)$(br)$(bold)Salt Ore:$() Can be enabled in config to generate naturally in river biomes.
piranport.book.cat.dungeon.desc: Sea area sorties and node-based combat.
piranport.book.dungeon.basics.p1: Obtain a $(item)Dungeon Key$() and find a $(item)Dungeon Lectern$(). Right-click the lectern with a key to open the sortie screen. Choose a chapter and stage, then press $(bold)Sortie$() to enter. Each stage has multiple nodes connected by $(item)Portals$(). Walk into a portal to advance to the next node.
piranport.book.dungeon.basics.p2: At each node, defeat all enemies to proceed. Collect supplies from $(item)Loot Ships$() along the way. If you fall in combat, you may revive by consuming a $(item)Totem of Undying$(), or give up and return to the lobby. Use a $(item)Town Scroll$() to exit the dungeon early. First clear of each stage grants bonus rewards. Your progress is saved on the $(item)Dungeon Key$().
message.piranport.no_fuel: No fuel! Use a fuel bucket to refuel
block.piranport.dungeon_lectern.no_key: You need a Dungeon Key to use this lectern
